title: K + R vs K
summary: Drive the king to the edge and deliver mate

steps.title: The Technique
steps.text:
## K + R vs K — the textbook win

King and rook against a lone king is **always winning** for the stronger side. There is no opposition trick to learn, no zugzwang exception — just a clean technique you can repeat from any starting position in **roughly 16 moves**.

### What this endgame looks like

- **Rook + king** versus a bare king.
- The board is empty; everything is about coordination.

### The key concept: cut and shoulder

The rook **cuts** off a rank or file the enemy king cannot cross. Your king then **shoulders** him — taking the opposition — to push him toward that wall. Mate arrives when the lone king is pinned to the **edge** with no escape squares.

### Procedure to convert

1. Place the rook to **cut off** the enemy king on a rank or file, narrowing his prison.
2. **March your king** up to face the enemy king (direct opposition).
3. When the enemy king must move along the wall, **swing the rook** to the new cut-off line.
4. Repeat until he is on the **edge**, then deliver mate with {{Rg8#}}-style cut.

### Common mistakes

- **Stalemating** the lone king on the edge with the rook far away. Always check that he has at least one square *or* he is in check.
- Allowing the kings to be adjacent on the same file — the king attacks your rook.
- Wandering the king while the rook does all the work — you need both.

> Rook cuts, king shoulders, then mate on the edge.

steps.title: The Mating Net
steps.text:
## The final pattern

Look carefully at this position. Black's king sits on **h8**, his own pawn on **h7** blocking the only escape. The White king on **f7** controls **g7** and **g8** — every flight square. White's rook on **g1** has a clear lane to **g8**.

### Why this is a forced mate

- **g8** — covered by White king *and* the rook delivering mate.
- **g7** — covered by White king on f7.
- **h7** — occupied by Black's own pawn.

The enemy king has nowhere to run. The mating piece, the rook, slides into **g8** with check.

### The pattern to memorise

- Defending king on the **edge** (8th rank).
- Attacking king **two squares away**, controlling the escape diagonally.
- Rook delivers **mate on the edge file or rank**.

This exact picture — sometimes with a pawn cooperating, sometimes pure — is the **fingerprint** of K+R vs K mate. Burn it in.

> Once you see the cage, you can build it from anywhere.

steps.prompt: Deliver **checkmate** along the back rank. The principle: *the rook closes the cage your king has already built*.
steps.hint: The h7 pawn is doing your work — slide the rook to the **8th rank** with check.
